title: Evangelion hw project help question
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion hw project help/question             Quote Post by zlink64 » Wed Dec 05, 2012 7:19 pm I got assignment where I need to write an summary of 5 philosphers we went over in class during semester. Then show their ideas in action through some kind of fiction or media. Any way having hard time because some of the readings I have to pick from aren't exactly type of stuff I imagine are used much in fiction. Any way I'm up to Hegel on my list and we read his "Master and Slave" essay from "the Phemonology of Spirit". I couldn't think of anything for few days and then BAM I remebered EOE ending where Shinji strangles Asuka. It reminded me about the slave master relationship. Then I though about how when Shinji goes through instrumentality it kinda seemed like he was going through the whole master-slave conflict with the world. Since he kinda killed them all and then was like "nah thats not a good idea". Then the whole idea "lets be one" matches Hegel's veiw of conciousness I think. Any way I don't exactly have the confidence to just make this connection since I haven't seen the show/movie in a few years. I'll rewatch later this week when I get a chance but I was wondering if anyone agreed/disagreed with my idea, had suggestion, or if they though I was miss interpreting the show or Hegel. So basically I just want some feed back prior to commiting to my choice. TY!

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Thu Dec 06, 2012 4:54 pm If anything, the most suitable candidates to represent the "master slave" relation you can find in Evangelion are the Evas. I don't think I need to explain why, but things like the incidents where they go berserk and/or try to attack their "masters" are good analogies to the concepts of rebellion and insubordination. I don't think, however, that you'd have an easy time relating and explaining Evangelion and Hegel, so I'd suggest you go with something more conventional. Dogville would be a good choice IMO.

[]:             Quote Post by zlink64 » Thu Dec 06, 2012 6:33 pm Never seen dogville will look into it though thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Fri Dec 07, 2012 3:22 am It's more subtle, but both the implied and the overt relationships between Gendo and Ritsuko are very much of a master/slave nature. The relationship between Gendo and Rei can also be seen as such. In a really strange way, the relationship between Kaji and Asuka or Kaji and Misato might be seen as such. However, I'm not really sure how well "enslaving oneself to another" really holds up on a logical basis.

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Fri Dec 07, 2012 3:26 am The strongest relation is Anno to Otaku culture, they're his slaves buying up all of the Evangelion products that come out each year and the master Anno sits on the piles of money raked in while criticizing them by subversive storytelling.
